title: Why New Zealand, the safe haven for wealthy Americans, is seeing an exodus of over-30s
summary:
“I never thought I’d live outside of Wellington, let alone New Zealand,” she told CNN from Australia, two years on.
Thorn’s more famous namesake, former Prime Minister Jacinda Ardern, recently became the unlikely face of this exodus.
The 45-year-old’s office confirmed last week that she and her family have relocated to Sydney, after they were spotted house-hunting in the city’s affluent northern beaches.
Jacinda Thorn relocated from Wellington to Melbourne with her husband Blair and their children Eva (left) and Chase (centre).
Darren Eckford and his family in Cornwall after their relocation from New Zealand to the United Kingdom.

title: ICE Just Abducted an Immigration Reporter in Nashville
summary:
On Tuesday morning, immigration reporter Estefany Rodríguez was in her marked news car with her husband outside of a gym in Nashville when several vehicles surrounded them, before a bunch of men jumped out to detain her, without any warrant.
When a journalist like Estefany Rodríguez is detained doing her job, the issue stops being about immigration and becomes about democracy itself.
— Identity is a Homeland 🇸🇩🕊️ (@mhmds_yd98458) March 6, 2026Rodríguez is a journalist for Nashville Noticias and Univision 42, and has been reporting on ICE operations in Nashville.
She joined Nashville Noticias in 2022.
ICE just grabbed NASHVILLE Noticias reporter Estefany Rodriguez outside of a gym.

title: At the border between Iran and Turkey, some flee the war, while others return
summary:
At the border between Iran and Turkey, some flee the war, while others returnIranians are streaming across the border with Turkey, fleeing constant bombardment.
(CROSSTALK)RUTH SHERLOCK, BYLINE: At this remote border crossing from Northern Iran, surrounded by snow-capped mountains, families arrive into Turkey looking exhausted and pale from all that they've lived through this past week.
But this man, without giving his name for fear of the regime, describes these days of war.
(SOUNDBITE OF ENGINE RUNNING)SHERLOCK: Many of the people on this border are here to cross into Iran, even now during the war.
Ruth Sherlock, NPR News, Turkey on the border with Iran.

title: Trump's border czar says he won't launch immigration blitz in NY, according to Gov. Hochul
summary:
Kathy Hochul left a meeting with President Donald Trump’s border czar on Friday believing the federal government won’t dramatically escalate immigration enforcement in the state without her blessing.
Hochul met with Tom Homan in her office at the state Capitol for nearly an hour.
It was an in-person, closed-door meeting the Democratic governor said was a follow-up to a previous phone call the two had about the federal government’s enforcement efforts.
Speaking to reporters afterward, Hochul said she reminded Homan that Trump had previously told her he wouldn’t launch a similar immigration enforcement blitz in New York unless she asked him to do so.
Homan did not engage, chewing an apple as state police escorted him to a nearby elevator.

title: Reporter detained for speaking Spanish in Nashville
summary:
Estefany Rodriguez Florez, a Colombian journalist with the Tennessee news outlet Nashville Noticias, was taken into custody Wednesday during a traffic stop in Nashville, according to court documents and statements from her attorney.
ICE officials say the arrest was part of an enforcement operation tied to alleged visa violations.
Court records filed by her lawyer say she also sought asylum after previously receiving death threats related to her reporting in Colombia.
The National Association of Hispanic Journalists condemned the arrest and called for her release so she can continue her work.
Her detention has prompted advocacy from press freedom and immigrant rights organizations, including the Committee to Protect Journalists, which urged authorities to release Rodriguez and ensure protections for reporters.

Naval Base at Pearl Harbor on Dec. 7, 1941.

title: Pro-Palestinian activist records questioning by German border police
summary: NewsFeed Pro-Palestinian activist records questioning by German border policePro-Palestinian German activist Yasemin Acar told Al Jazeera about what she says was harassment at a Berlin airport where she recorded a border guard asking about her destination because of concerns over “hostility towards Israel”.

title: Rep Henry Cuellar Calls Out Texas Democratic Senate Nominee James Talarico Over ‘Welcome Mat’ At Border Remarks
summary:
Democratic Texas Rep. Henry Cuellar said Saturday on Fox News he hopes James Talarico, his state party’s nominee for U.S. Senate, changes his stated view that the southern border should have a “giant welcome mat” for migrants.
“Saturday in America” host Kayleigh McEnany asked the congressman if he agreed with Talarico’s border remarks, which he made during a Democratic primary debate with Texas Rep. Jasmine Crockett.
Cuellar replied, “No, of course I don’t.”“I hope I spend some time with him [Talarico] and get him to change the situation at the border.
And I live at the border, I just don’t go visit the border,” Cuellar told McEnany.
“What is it going to take to get your colleagues on board because we need DHS right now?” the Fox News host asked Cuellar.

title: Appeals Court Affirms: Trump Can Suspend Refugee Resettlement to U.S.
summary:
The United States Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit affirmed this week that President Donald Trump is within full presidential authority to suspend refugee resettlement to the U.S.
The panel of judges, the largest in the nation for an appellate court, vacated a lower court’s injunction against the Trump administration’s executive order suspending refugee resettlement.
[Emphasis added]Dale Wilcox with the Federation for American Immigration Reform (FAIR) celebrated the decision as the group had filed a brief in support of Trump’s authority to suspend refugee resettlement.
We are pleased the court recognized the President’s prerogative and vacated the lower court’s activist injunction.”The case is Pacito v. Trump, No.
2:25-cv-00255-JNW in the United States Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit.
